Mod: Accurate Block Placement v1.1.16
Description: Makes block placing/breaking smoother and more consistent
Notes: Once you try it, you can't play without it

Description: Completely overhauls the F3 menu
Notes: Can be a bit overwhelming initially

Description: Aids in measuring block distances
Notes: Gets very buggy at extreme XYZ

Description: Utilizes camera tilting in relation to movement
Notes: Can be fairly trippy initially, but you'll get used to it.

Description: Enables custom capes from a variety of sources
Notes: This version is marked as 1.19.4, but it works on 1.20.1

Description: Audio and visual notifications for when specified words or phrases appear in chat
Notes: Does what it says, no bloat

Description: Lets you fly faster in Creative mode
Notes: Have you ever zoomed at the speed of light? Do you WANT to zoom at the speed of light?

Mod: Dark Loading Screen v1.6.14
Description: Makes the initial loading screen darker (as opposed to the default red/white)
Notes: If you're sick of having your eyeballs burned by the loading screen, you need this

Description: Fixes sprint jumping with an elytra in a 2 block high tunnel
Notes: Small QoL feature, but very awesome nonetheless

Description: Bounce off the ground instead of landing when using elytra
Notes: Meshes very well with Squake/Re-Squake

Description: Allows you to easily type emoji and emoticons in game
Notes: :skull: :skull: :skull:

Description: Forces the first argument of commands typed in chat to be lowercase
Notes: Small QoL feature, but very awesome nonetheless

Description: Memory usage optimizations
Notes: Can't say I've really seen the effects of it, but... the o p t i m i z a t i o n

Description: Loads of useful utilities, mostly relating to NBT
Notes: I cannot possibly recommend this mod enough, if you like making cool stuff, you need FZMM

Description: Paginates the vanilla hotbar system to infinitely increase storage space
Notes: Ever wanted more than one page of saved hotbars? This is the mod for you :D

Description: Simple GUI Mod to edit an item, block or entity's NBT data
Notes: Easily the best in-game NBT Editor, 10/10 UI

Description: Speed up immediate mode rendering in Minecraft
Notes: Massive FPS increase when lots of entities are around (compared to vanilla)

Description: Modern shaders mod for Minecraft intended to be compatible with existing OptiFine shader packs
Notes: Does it need a note? It's Iris dude...

Description: Macro and Scripting mod supporting many events and triggers
Notes: I literally only use it for cheating at reaction events, but hey it works great for it!

Description: Schematic mod reminiscent of Schematica, but for newer versions
Notes: I have no way to properly express how amazing this mod is

Description: Fixes random memory leaks for both the client and server
Notes: Can't say for certain, but pretty sure it's prevented serious issues for me many times over

Mod: Meteor Client v0.5.4-1862
Description: Hack/Cheat client
Notes: Might not be the best (or most stable) client available, but it's still my favorite <3

Mod: Meteor Litematica Printer v1.2
Description: Fast printer for Litematica without rubberbanding
Notes: Obviously requires Meteor Client, but is very well done; Just don't try and print blockstates, unless you want a hellscape

Description: Design elaborate buildings within a minute!
Notes: This is peak modding... you can actually generate half decent buildings in seconds, with infinite variety

Description: Egregious, yet effective performance improvements for modern Minecraft
Notes: Speeds up loading times. S p e e d . . .

Description: Adds a menu to view and configure the mods you have installed
Notes: It's Mod Menu, 'nuff said

Description: Client side mod for editing and storing items
Notes: What NBTE lacks in UI, it more than makes up for in utility; It's also required in order to use my Shulker Archives

Description: Logs every item containing NBT to a creative menu tab
Notes: Except that it doesn't currently support InfNaN tags, this is an amazing mod for auto-yoinking items from unsuspecting players

Mod: No Chat Reports v2.2.2
Description: Makes chat unreportable (where possible)
Notes: Imagine submitting to daddy mojang and making your messages reportable 💀

Description: Prevents the player from drifting after they stopped flying in creative mode
Notes: Can't go back to vanilla after using this; WHY Mojang thought un-toggleable drift should be a thing is beyond my understanding

Description: A simple mod that disables the narrator feature in Minecraft
Notes: Get fucked Narrator, nobody likes your robotic ass voice being accidentally enabled because a common key-bind got pressed

Description: An advanced client side Block, Entity, NBT, Tool and ID picker
Notes: Expands the default pick-block feature; Incredibly useful when working with NBT

Description: Allows you to record, replay and share your Minecraft gameplay
Notes: I rarely use ReplayMod, but ever since it stopped requiring a special version of Sodium I've just kept it ¯\_(ツ)_/¯

Description: Rewrite of a popular mod which brings quake-style movement to Minecraft
Notes: *smoovement intensifies*

Description: Makes resource packs load in the background!
Notes: I never realized how much I needed the ability to do things while resources load until I got this mod

Mod: Smooth Menu Refabricated v1.0.0
Description: Removes the main menu FPS limit
Notes: "Dear Mojang, WHY?! Why would you add an un-toggleable FPS limit in Menus?!?"

Description: Prevents menus from un-sneaking the player
Notes: "Dear Mojang, WHY?! Why on earth would opening a menu un-sneak you?!?"

Description: A modern rendering engine for Minecraft which greatly improves frame rates and micro-stutter, while fixing graphical issues
Notes: Do we really need Sodium now that Minecraft v1.19.4+ has better performance? Idk.

Description: Plays MIDI & NBS songs with noteblocks
Notes: This mod was cool enough at launch, but v3.0+ has taken it to new heights of amazing

Description: Adds a symbol/emoji menu as well as writing in different unicode fonts
Notes: Gone are the days of looking through random blogs for lists of symbols to use in Minecraft

Description: Client-side mod containing multiple utilities for extremely niche uses
Notes: It might be niche, but it's still incredibly useful and incredibly based

Description: Host your singleplayer worlds without having to run a server or setup port forwarding!
Notes: Can't say I've ever really used this, but I'm sure I will at some point

Description: A Minecraft Map Editor... that runs in-game!
Notes: If you don't know what this mod does, please get off this site. Now.

Description: Displays the nearby world terrain, players, mobs, entities in the corner of your screen
Notes: I hate Xaero's map mods, but there's no better alternative sadly

Description: Adds a full screen world map which shows you what you have explored in the world
Notes: I hate Xaero's map mods, but there's no better alternative sadly

Description: Disables the Experimental Settings popup, which appears when you create or load world
Notes: "...'Yeetus Experimentus' yelled Steve Potter, as he brandished his wand at Minecraft..."
